IND:Megatron-LM@300d1b6550b4->e836e6225f.mechanism: In TECudaGraphHelper.__init__ (megatron/core/transformer/cuda_graphs.py), the MTP-layer collection loop for --external-cuda-graph capture accessed `chunk_with_decoder.mtp.layers[layer_number].transformer_layer` to pick the callable to hand to TE make_graphed_callables(). That submodule was renamed from `transformer_layer` to `mtp_model_layer` when MTP support for hybrid (mamba/transformer) models was added (#2363/#3207), so for hybrid models the wrong/nonexistent attribute was read, meaning MTP layers were not correctly captured into CUDA graphs. The fix reads `.mtp_model_layer`. (The second hunk in training.py only guards `args.mtp_num_layers=None` in the FLOP-count utility — a metrics-only fix, not a runtime perf path.)

IND:Megatron-LM@66da23bff9e7->e1a9ac94a0.mechanism: PersistentAsyncCaller.async_loop is decorated with @_disable_gc(), so garbage collection is disabled for the entire lifetime of the persistent checkpoint worker process. Each iteration binds `item` (an AsyncRequest) and `async_fn_args` (a list that includes CPU-staged state-dict tensors returned by preload_fn()). These objects participate in reference cycles that pure refcounting cannot reclaim; with GC disabled they are never collected, so staged checkpoint tensors/host memory accumulate across successive saves. The fix adds `del async_fn_args`, `del item`, and an explicit `gc.collect()` per loop iteration to force reclamation despite the disabled cyclic collector.

manifest_conditions.feature: async/persistent distributed checkpointing (PersistentAsyncCaller), repeated checkpoint saves over long training

IND:Megatron-LM@c8ac1fedb7b3->43b4471d1e.mechanism: Reverts feature PR #2738 ("Evict and re-compute context requests") in the dynamic-inference KV-cache scheduler. #2738 had split the KV buffer into equal active/paused halves (BlockAllocator: `active_count=(total_count-1)//2`, buffer `2*buffer_size_gb`) and made `is_memory_available` gate on `get_active_avail()>=num_blocks` (only ~50% of blocks usable for active requests) plus introduced eviction/re-compute of paused requests. The revert restores the prior model where the full buffer is usable for active requests (`is_memory_available` returns `total_avail>=num_blocks`) and a separately-sized `paused_buffer_size_gb`, plus `max_requests`/`max_total_requests`/`max_active_requests` naming. Since this is a revert, it restores the pre-#2738 concurrency/scheduling behavior — presumably because #2738 regressed or destabilized inference throughput/memory, though the revert message states no reason.

manifest_conditions.parallelism: dynamic-batching inference; pp_size>1 all-reduces block_count; tp_size rounds max_requests
manifest_conditions.hardware: GPU KV cache; unified_memory_level 1 uses CPU for paused blocks
manifest_conditions.shape: KV block buffer sizing (buffer_size_gb, block_size_tokens, max_sequence_length)

IND:vllm@107cf8e92f88->e27078ea807c.mechanism: ROCm attention backend's get_supported_kernel_block_sizes() returned only [16, 32], excluding the non-standard block_size=544 required by Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B. This forced the model off the optimized Triton paged-attention kernel path (fallback), causing a performance regression. The fix adds 544 to the supported list and fixes the Triton kernel so the non-standard model uses an optimized kernel path while standard models keep the original bit-addressing logic.

IND:vllm@285bab47526c->047a457fa4af.mechanism: Qwen3_5GatedDeltaNet fully overrides Qwen3NextGatedDeltaNet.__init__ and, before this commit, never set self.chunk_gated_delta_rule. PR #32846 (merged the same day) refactored the shared GDN prefill call site from the free function `fla_chunk_gated_delta_rule(...)` to `self.chunk_gated_delta_rule(...)`, where ChunkGatedDeltaRule is a CustomOp that dispatches to the FlashInfer GDN prefill kernel (forward_cuda) on CUDA compute capability 90 and to Triton/FLA otherwise. Because Qwen3.5's overridden __init__ lacked the attribute, Qwen3.5 could not run the intended prefill dispatch (would AttributeError / fail to use the FlashInfer path). This commit adds `self.chunk_gated_delta_rule = ChunkGatedDeltaRule()` so Qwen3.5 selects the FlashInfer GDN prefill kernel on Hopper, matching Qwen3-Next.

manifest_conditions.hardware: NVIDIA Hopper / CUDA compute capability 90 (SM90) for the FlashInfer fast path
manifest_conditions.model: Qwen3.5 / Qwen3.5-MoE (gated delta net linear-attention layers)

IND:vllm@73391a1baa45->d0cd736caada.mechanism: The multimodal processor and the AsyncMicrobatchTokenizer shared the same underlying HuggingFace Rust fast-tokenizer instance. Under concurrent load, simultaneous access from call_hf_processor and the async tokenizer triggered a Rust RefCell "RuntimeError: Already borrowed", causing request failures/contention that degraded VLM serving throughput. The fix `mm_tokenizer = copy.deepcopy(tokenizer)` gives the mm processor its own tokenizer backend, eliminating the cross-path borrow conflict.

manifest_conditions.tokenizer: HuggingFace fast/Rust-backed tokenizer with internal RefCell

IND:vllm@75e01a39a16c->ed733802f044.mechanism: The NUMA-binding feature auto-detects a GPU's NUMA node via nvmlDeviceGetNumaNodeId and pins the worker subprocess with `numactl --cpunodebind=<node> --membind=<node>` (numa_utils._get_numactl_args). On non-CDMM Grace-Blackwell (GB200) systems each GPU's HBM is its own NUMA node with NO CPUs, so NVML returns a CPU-less node; binding CPU/memory affinity to that node yields wrong/ineffective worker placement, defeating the locality optimization NUMA binding exists to provide. The fix adds `_numa_node_has_cpus(numa_node)` and, when the NVML node has no CPUs, falls through to CPU-affinity-based detection to pick the nearest real CPU node.

manifest_conditions.hardware: non-CDMM Grace-Blackwell / GB200 (per-GPU HBM NUMA nodes without CPUs)
manifest_conditions.parallelism: multi-GPU workers with --numa-bind enabled (auto node detection)

IND:vllm@889722f3bfda->0aac2048bf3a.mechanism: Full CUDA graph capture/replay requires input tensors at stable device addresses. In the FP8 FlashMLA decode path, get_mla_metadata_dense_fp8 allocates fresh tile_scheduler_metadata/num_splits tensors every step; without copying them into the persistent cg_buf_* buffers, the captured graph reads freshly-allocated (non-persistent) pointers, breaking full-cudagraph decode for FP8 KV-cache. The fix re-adds the copy_() into self.cg_buf_tile_scheduler_metadata/self.cg_buf_num_splits (guarded by cudagraph_mode.has_full_cudagraphs()), restoring the persistent-buffer contract so the fast full-CUDA-graph decode path works again.

manifest_conditions.hardware: SM90 (Hopper) / SM100 (Blackwell) — FlashMLA supports_compute_capability major in [9,10]

IND:vllm@8b141ed8c328->56e19d7ee206.mechanism: The FlexAttention backend's persistent CUDA-graph buffers were mis-sized. Before the fix, `persistent_kv_indices` was allocated as `torch.empty(self.max_model_len, max_num_kv_block)` with `max_num_kv_block = ceil(max_model_len/kv_block_size)` — i.e. quadratic in max_model_len (e.g. 131072 x ~8192 int32 ≈ 16GB persistent GPU allocation), while the runtime `kv_indices` tensor it backs only needs `max_num_query_groups x (q_block_size * pages_per_seq)`. `persistent_kv_num_blocks` first dim was also wrongly `max_num_q_block` (derived from max_model_len) instead of `max_num_query_groups = cdiv(max_num_batched_tokens, q_block_size)`. The fix replaces both with correctly-sized allocations, eliminating the over-allocation and fixing the shape mismatch that `copy_to_persistent` re-strides against.

IND:vllm@8ee90c83f8e8->45f526d65237.mechanism: `_max_memory_usage_bytes_from_groups` in kv_cache_utils.py computed `blocks_needed` from only the first KV-cache group's spec (`any_spec = kv_cache_groups[0].kv_cache_spec`), then multiplied by group_size*page_size. For models with multiple heterogeneous KV-cache groups (e.g. hybrid Mamba + full-attention, per the added `new_mamba_spec` test), this under-counts total per-token memory usage. Because this value feeds the `--max-model-len auto` feature that sizes context to available memory, the underestimate would let auto-fit pick a max_model_len whose KV cache does not actually fit in available memory (OOM / mis-sized budget). The fix sums cdiv(spec.max_memory_usage_bytes(...), page_size) over all groups.

manifest_conditions.shape: hybrid models with multiple heterogeneous KV-cache groups (e.g. Mamba + full-attention); no-op for single-group / uniform-group models

IND:vllm@cc06b4e86b2b->97f92c6b4741.mechanism: The prior commit cc06b4e86 (#38270) added a CUDA-graph profiling path that calls `initialize_kv_cache(minimal_config, is_profiling=True)` to build a throwaway "minimal KV cache for CUDA graph profiling", threading `is_profiling` through initialize_attn_backend and _check_and_update_cudagraph_mode. But it left the KV-transfer-connector registration block inside initialize_kv_cache unguarded, so when a KV connector is active the connector's `register_kv_caches`/`register_cross_layers_kv_cache` + `set_host_xfer_buffer_ops` ran against the minimal profiling cache that is immediately discarded — redundant (and semantically wrong) registration work during startup. This fix gates that block with `and not is_profiling` to skip it during the profiling dummy run.

manifest_conditions.parallelism: requires an active KV transfer connector (has_kv_transfer_group), i.e. disaggregated prefill/decode setups (e.g. NIXL)
manifest_conditions.hardware: GPU (gpu_model_runner); connector registration may involve host xfer buffers / RDMA memory registration

IND:vllm@cd4a95e3aa04->a01ef3fa51a0.mechanism: In HybridKVCacheCoordinator.find_longest_cache_hit, the iterative fixed-point loop restarts over all attention groups whenever any group reduces the hit length. With EAGLE enabled on a simple hybrid model (1 full-attn + 1 sliding-window group, e.g. gpt-oss), the non-full-attn manager applies its EAGLE last-block drop on every pass, so repeated iterations 'spiral' the hit length down to 0 — prefix caching is completely defeated and prompt tokens are recomputed each request (hit rate == 0). The fix adds `is_simple_hybrid` detection (2 groups, first is FullAttentionSpec) and breaks after a single iteration, and moves full-attention block truncation to a single post-loop step so the EAGLE drop is applied only once. FIXME notes the multi-group 'EAGLE spiral block dropping' problem remains (issue #32802).
IND:vllm@cd4a95e3aa04->a01ef3fa51a0.magnitude: prefix cache hit rate == 0 (commit subject "[Fix] prefix cache hit rate == 0 bug with gpt-oss style models"); no numeric speedup quoted

manifest_conditions.model: hybrid attention: 1 full-attn + 1 sliding-window group (gpt-oss style)

IND:vllm@d6e04f4c4361->cc06b4e86b2b.mechanism: The prior fix d6e04f4c4 (#34094) fixed a Mamba out-of-bounds by calling adjust_cudagraph_sizes_for_mamba_cache(num_blocks), which silently shrank the shared compilation_config.cudagraph_capture_sizes / max_cudagraph_capture_size to num_blocks. Because these lists are shared, capping them also removed larger capture sizes for PIECEWISE (prefill) cudagraphs, forcing those batch sizes to run eagerly instead of via CUDA graphs (extra launch overhead / bubbles). This commit deletes the capping and instead raises a ValueError when max_num_seqs > num_blocks, relying on the decode dispatcher's existing max_num_seqs cap for FULL graphs while leaving PIECEWISE graph sizes unrestricted.

IND:vllm@ebb9cc5f2b26->79eb9369c5ba.mechanism: In determine_available_memory(), the CUDA graph memory estimate (cudagraph_memory_estimate_applied) was added into self.peak_activation_memory, which then flows into non_kv_cache_memory (non_kv_cache_memory = model_memory_usage + peak_activation_memory + ...). But available_kv_cache_memory_bytes = requested_memory - non_kv_cache_memory - cudagraph_memory_estimate_applied ALSO subtracts the estimate explicitly. So the CUDA graph memory was subtracted twice, underestimating available KV cache memory → smaller KV cache → lower max concurrency/throughput. The fix sets peak_activation_memory = torch_peak_increase only, leaving the single explicit subtraction.

manifest_conditions.config: requires VLLM_MEMORY_PROFILER_ESTIMATE_CUDAGRAPHS=1 and not enforce_eager; off by default

Megatron-LM@4ba9f46967e3.mechanism: The prior commit de56227b2 (#2652, one day earlier) wrapped the ENTIRE model-init path (build_model, `.cuda()` GPU allocation, Float16Module fp16 conversion, meta-device materialization, DDP wrap) in a side CUDA stream `setup_stream` to fix a race. Running allocation/conversion off the default stream causes the caching allocator to tag those allocations to the side stream and forces the default stream to wait on all of it, adding init-time serialization/side effects. This commit narrows the side stream scope so model construction/allocation/fp16 conversion run on the default (current) stream, and only the DDP wrapping (the part that actually needs the side stream for cuda-graph capture with DDP) stays off-stream via `ddp_stream`, with the same `wait_stream` sync retained.

manifest_conditions.parallelism: DDP / FSDP data-parallel init; cuda-graph capture path

vllm@05dc4bfab697.mechanism: The new Model Runner V2 (vllm/v1/worker/gpu/model_runner.py) was missing the post-model-load call to prepare_communication_buffer_for_model(self.model), which the older V1 runner (gpu_model_runner.py) already made. That function lets MoE all2all device communicators (EP/DP path) allocate model-shape-specific communication buffers; its docstring states these buffers (e.g. DeepEP) are allocated 'based on the model shape for optimal performance.' Without the call, the EP group's all2all_impl is never prepared on V2, so the perf-optimized all2all buffer/path is not set up (and for the naive backend the dispatch/combine asserts self.all2all_impl is not None). The commit ports the call (and a speculator-model variant) into V2, restoring the buffer initialization.

manifest_conditions.parallelism: data-parallel + expert-parallel (MoE all2all, e.g. DeepEP/naive backend), VLLM_ALL2ALL_BACKEND set
manifest_conditions.model: MoE models using FusedMoE all2all dispatch/combine

vllm@39f9ea0da4a4.mechanism: In pure `cudagraph_mode=FULL`, dispatch of a uniform-decode batch failed to find the registered FULL cudagraph key, so it fell back to PIECEWISE/NONE (no full-graph replay). FULL keys are registered via the mixed_mode loop with `_create_padded_batch_descriptor(bs, uniform_decode=False, ...)`, where `num_reqs = min(num_tokens_padded, max_num_seqs)`. At dispatch the old code built the descriptor with `uniform_decode=True` (giving `num_reqs = num_tokens_padded // uniform_decode_query_len`) and then only flipped `uniform` back to False via `replace(batch_desc, uniform=False)`, leaving the mismatched `num_reqs`. The lookup `batch_desc_to_check in self.cudagraph_keys[FULL]` therefore missed. The fix computes `normalized_uniform = uniform_decode and self.cudagraph_mode.separate_routine()` (False in pure FULL, which has no separate routine) before building the descriptor, so `num_reqs` matches the registered key and the FULL graph is dispatched. Default `FULL_AND_PIECEWISE` has `separate_routine()`=True so it was unaffected.

vllm@6a9cceb219fc.mechanism: AiterExperts.expects_unquantized_inputs always returned True, which drove the modular MoE framework to request defer_input_quant=True from the prepare step; but MoriPrepareAndFinalize.prepare() raised NotImplementedError for defer_input_quant=True, blocking the MoRI+AITER FP8 combination. The fix makes expects_unquantized_inputs return False when use_mori_kernels is set and skips FP8 dispatch quantization only when defer_input_quant is set, so MoRI now quantizes tokens to FP8 before the all2all dispatch. Per the added comment this "handles FP8 quantization during dispatch to reduce network traffic" — i.e. it enables the reduced-bandwidth interconnect path for expert-parallel dispatch instead of dispatching unquantized activations.

vllm@7c94ae16c6c2.mechanism: With --max-model-len=-1 (auto), EngineCore reduces max_model_len after KV-cache memory profiling ("auto-fit"), but the reduced value was never propagated back to the frontend: the ready handshake sent an empty message (`input_socket.send(b"")`). The frontend therefore validated prompts against the original (large) max_model_len, accepted oversized requests, and forwarded them to the engine, which could not schedule them within the tiny auto-fitted KV budget — the request hangs indefinitely and starves the entire service. The fix introduces `EngineCoreReadyResponse` carrying `max_model_len`, sends it as the ready payload, and `_apply_ready_response` clamps the frontend's `model_config.max_model_len` to the engine's value so oversized prompts are rejected up front (VLLMValidationError "Please reduce the length").

manifest_conditions.shape: triggered when max_model_len=-1 (auto) and KV-cache budget forces auto-fit to a small value; prompts exceeding the auto-fitted length

vllm@8a5e0e2b2bb9.mechanism: Request.__init__ stored `self.get_hash_new_full_blocks = partial(block_hasher, self)`, binding the Request into a partial held as its own attribute — a Request->partial->Request reference cycle. This prevents immediate reference-count-based deallocation when a Request finishes, so Request objects (and their token/block-hash lists) accumulate as CPU memory until Python's cyclic GC runs, i.e. a CPU memory leak in prefix caching. The fix stores the hasher unbound (`self._block_hasher = block_hasher`) and passes `self` at call time via new `update_block_hashes()`, breaking the cycle.

vllm@a0638d052db7.mechanism: PR #32993 refactored get_accelerator_view_from_cpu_tensor, replacing the unconditional fall-through `return torch.ops._C.get_cuda_view_from_cpu_tensor(...)` with `elif current_platform.is_cuda(): ... else: raise ValueError`. On ROCm, is_cuda() is False (platform reports as rocm), so the UVA CPU weight-offloading path raised ValueError instead of taking the CUDA/HIP view path it previously used. This fix adds `or current_platform.is_rocm()` to route ROCm back through get_cuda_view_from_cpu_tensor (HIP shares the cudaHostGetDevicePointer runtime API in cuda_view.cu), restoring UVA-based CPU weight offloading — which the #32993 diff notes is required for cudagraph and reduces memory/allocation overhead.

vllm@ea5f903f80fe.mechanism: Commit 23d825aba (#34392) disabled the AllReduce+RMSNorm+quant torch.compile fusion for DeepSeekV3 under NVFP4 quantization because the fused path produced incorrect results (issue #34395), forcing the model back onto separate all-reduce, RMSNorm, and quant kernels. This commit bumps FlashInfer 0.6.3→0.6.4 (which fixes the underlying fusion bug) and removes the DeepseekV3ForCausalLM config override that set pass_config.fuse_allreduce_rms=False, re-enabling the fused collective+norm+quant path and restoring the reduced kernel-launch/communication latency.

vllm@f26fcdfb9e50.mechanism: Two helper functions (`paged_mqa_logits_module`, `mqa_logits_module`) were defined *inside* `rocm_fp8_paged_mqa_logits`/`rocm_fp8_mqa_logits` and decorated with `@functools.lru_cache`. Because the nested `def` creates a fresh function object (with a brand-new empty cache) on every call to the enclosing function, the `lru_cache` never persisted across calls, so the expensive aiter module resolution (`importlib.util.find_spec` up to twice plus `importlib.import_module`) re-ran on every sparse-attn indexer invocation instead of once. Moving both functions to module scope makes the `lru_cache` effective, so module lookup/import happens only once per process.

manifest_conditions.note: paged path is additionally force-disabled by a FIXME, so live impact is mainly rocm_fp8_mqa_logits prefill path

vllm@ffc0a2798b11.mechanism: The DeepEP low-latency dispatch call in prepare_async() was missing round_scale=self.use_ue8m0_dispatch and use_ue8m0=self.use_ue8m0_dispatch. self.use_ue8m0_dispatch is set True by post_init_setup() when the fused_experts backend supports_packed_ue8m0_act_scales() (the DeepGEMM packed-ue8m0 activation-scale path on Blackwell). Without these kwargs, buffer.low_latency_dispatch() defaults to non-ue8m0 scale format, silently bypassing the packed ue8m0 dispatch optimization that the backend was set up to use. This commit adds the two params back to re-enable the packed path.

manifest_conditions.dtype: fp8 blockwise with packed ue8m0 activation scales
